query: top 3 smartphones in 2025, compare pricing across US marketplaces
answer_text: Here are three of the strongest smartphones in 2025: iPhone 16, Samsung Galaxy S25, and Google Pixel 9a. For US marketplace pricing, the best available figures I found show starting MSRP from a review source plus used-market averages from Swappa for comparable models.swappa+1Pricing snapshotPhoneWhy it stands out in 2025Starting priceSwappa avg. used priceApple iPhone 16Best all-around pick for most people businessinsider$799 businessinsider$613 swappaSamsung Galaxy S25Top Android flagship pick businessinsider$799.99 businessinsider$469 swappaGoogle Pixel 9aBest budget Android choice phonearena$499 phonearenaNot listed in the Swappa data I found swappaHow they compareThe iPhone 16 is the safest premium choice if you want broad ecosystem support and strong battery life, and its launch price is in line with the Galaxy S25.businessinsiderThe Galaxy S25 is similarly priced at launch, but the used-market average is notably lower than the iPhone 16 in the pricing data I found.swappa+1The Pixel 9a is the clear value pick at $499, making it much cheaper upfront than either flagship while still being positioned as a top 2025 budget phone.phonearenaMarketplace angleFor new-phone pricing, retail/MSRP is the cleanest comparison across US sellers, and both the iPhone 16 and Galaxy S25 launched at about $800.businessinsiderFor secondary marketplaces, Swappa shows the Galaxy S25 averaging $469 used and the iPhone 16 averaging $613 used, which suggests the Samsung model is cheaper on resale right now.swappaThat makes the Galaxy S25 the lowest-cost flagship of the three in the used market, while the Pixel 9a remains the lowest-cost option overall at launch.phonearena+1Best pick by budgetBest overall: iPhone 16.businessinsiderBest Android flagship: Galaxy S25.businessinsiderBest value: Pixel 9a.phonearenaI can also turn this into a cleaner US marketplace comparison with separate columns for Amazon, Best Buy, Walmart, and Swappa if you want a tighter retail-by-retail view.
